strengths: Successfully improved performance by tightening the threshold to an extreme 0.98: Aggregate Win Rate increased to 89.7% (from 89.2% in parent spec) over a large sample of 6,653 trades.
Achieved a very strong positive expectancy of +6.60 bps per trade, an improvement over the parent's +6.41 bps, confirming the profitability of this highly selective signal.
Maintains a substantial number of trades (6,653) even at this extreme threshold, demonstrating the signal is not impractically rare.
weaknesses: Performance remains mixed across symbols. The average Win Rate for symbol 000660 (91.1%) is 2.2 percentage points higher than for 005930 (88.9%), indicating persistent symbol-specific behavior.
The trade count was halved from 13,077 to 6,653 compared to the parent spec (iter006_obi1_gt_095_h50), suggesting diminishing returns from further threshold tightening.
An extremely high win rate of 89.7% may indicate sensitivity to backtest idealizations (e.g., zero latency, perfect fills) that could degrade performance in a live environment.
recommended_direction_reasoning: This spec confirms a powerful candidate edge, improving both Win Rate to 89.7% and expectancy to +6.60 bps over 6,653 trades. The trend shows that tightening the OBI threshold consistently improves precision. The next logical step is to push this to its absolute limit (e.g., 0.99) to determine the signal's performance ceiling and isolate the highest possible conviction events, even if it significantly reduces trade frequency.
